# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim-buster

# Cache for faster builds
RUN apk --no-cache add --virtual .build-deps gcc libffi-dev musl-dev

# Copy the requirements file and install dependencies
COPY requirements.txt /app/
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r /app/requirements.txt

# Move the application code into the container
COPY . /app

# Work directory
WORKDIR /app

# Expose app port (you may need to adjust this according to your needs)
EXPOSE 8000

# Entry point script for running the application
ENTRYPOINT ["python", "app.py"]
